info.title: Tori API
info.description:
Managed recurring billing engine built natively on Nomba. Start a subscription with one API call. Tori handles everything after — dunning, renewals, recovery, ledger, and customer self-service.

This spec documents the integration surface only: the Platform API you call from your own server, the webhooks Tori sends back, and the Customer Portal your customers can authenticate into directly. It does not document the Tori dashboard's own internal endpoints — those exist to power the dashboard UI, not for third-party integration.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Obtaining an access token or dashboard session

tags.name: Platform API — Subscriptions
tags.description: Start, list, and manage subscriptions from your own server

tags.name: Platform API — Customers
tags.description: Look up customers and generate self-service portal access

tags.name: Platform API — Plans
tags.description: Read-only access to your pricing plans

tags.name: Platform API — Payment Links
tags.description: Collect one-off payments with no subscription involved

tags.name: Platform API — Customer Email
tags.description: Trigger a one-off transactional email to a customer

tags.name: Customer Portal
tags.description: What your customers call directly to self-serve their own billing

post.description: Exchanges OAuth client credentials for a short-lived bearer token, usable anywhere an X-API-Key is accepted. An alternative to a static API key — use whichever fits your integration; both authenticate the same Platform API surface.

error.message: request body is not valid JSON
invalid client credentials
the requested resource does not exist

post.summary: Log in as a tenant operator
post.description: Dashboard/operator login — not used by customer-facing integrations. Returns a JWT pair scoped to the tenant.

error.message: request body is not valid JSON
invalid credentials
the requested resource does not exist

post.description: Creates a customer and subscription, returns a Nomba checkout URL to redirect the customer to. Finds or creates the customer by email — idempotent via idempotency_key embedded in metadata handling on retries of the same request.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: A unique key (UUID recommended) to make this request safe to retry. If Tori has seen this key before, it replays the original response without re-executing the operation. Keys are scoped per tenant and expire after 24 hours.

error.message: plan_id is required
invalid or missing API key
the requested resource does not exist

post.summary: Create a checkout for a payment link
post.description: Collects a one-time payment via a Nomba checkout page — no subscription or plan involved. A payment link can also be shared as a bare, unauthenticated URL at POST /v1/payment-links/{id}/pay if you'd rather not integrate this call at all.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: A unique key (UUID recommended) to make this request safe to retry. If Tori has seen this key before, it replays the original response without re-executing the operation. Keys are scoped per tenant and expire after 24 hours.

error.message: this payment link is no longer active
invalid or missing API key
the requested resource does not exist

post.summary: Send a transactional email to a customer
post.description: Sends a one-off email to a specific customer — either one of your configured merchant billing templates (subscription.activated, payment.succeeded, etc.), or fully custom subject/HTML with {{variable}} placeholders.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: A unique key (UUID recommended) to make this request safe to retry. If Tori has seen this key before, it replays the original response without re-executing the operation. Keys are scoped per tenant and expire after 24 hours.

template.description: One of the 7 supported merchant billing event types — uses your configured or default copy for it

custom_subject.description: Required together with custom_html if not using a template

custom_html.description: Required together with custom_subject if not using a template

variables.description: Values substituted into {{key}} placeholders in custom_subject/custom_html, or into known fields (plan_name, amount_kobo, next_billing_date, pay_link) when using template

error.message: either template, or custom_subject and custom_html, is required
invalid or missing API key
the requested resource does not exist

post.description: Sends a 6-digit code to the customer's email. Rate limited to 3 requests per email per 10 minutes.

tenant_id.description: Not currently required by the API — a customer email is looked up across all tenants — but reserved for a future tenant-scoped variant of this flow

200.description: Always 200 regardless of whether the email matches a customer, so this endpoint can't be used to enumerate customer emails

data.message: If this email is associated with a subscription, a login code has been sent.

error.message: request body is not valid JSON
invalid or missing API key

429.description: Rate limited — too many requests for this email

error.message: too many OTP requests — try again in 10 minutes

post.description: Exchanges a valid, unexpired, unused code for a portal session token. Capped at 5 failed attempts per email per 15 minutes — once hit, every subsequent attempt is rejected regardless of whether the code entered is correct, until a fresh code is requested.

tenant_id.description: Not currently required by the API — see request-otp

otp.description: The actual request body field is named code, not otp

error.message: this code is invalid or has expired
invalid or missing API key

429.description: Too many failed attempts — the OTP is locked out

error.message: OTP invalidated — request a new code
request body is not valid JSON
invalid or expired portal token
the requested resource does not exist
request body is not valid JSON
invalid or expired portal token
the requested resource does not exist

post.description: Always cancels at period end — access continues until current_period_end.

error.message: request body is not valid JSON
subscription does not belong to this customer
the requested resource does not exist

apiKeyAuth.name: X-API-Key
apiKeyAuth.description: Your Tori API key. Use tori_live_ for production, tori_test_ for sandbox.

bearerAuth.description: OAuth 2.0 Bearer token from POST /v1/oauth/token, or dashboard JWT from POST /v1/auth/login

data.description: Shape varies by event_type — see the Webhooks section

x-webhooks.description: Tori dispatches these events to every webhook endpoint you register, subscribed to that event type (or "*" for all). Every webhook request carries an X-Tori-Signature header — HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body, hex-encoded and prefixed "sha256=", using the signing secret shown once when the endpoint was registered. Verify it with a timing-safe comparison before trusting the payload.

subscription.activated.fires_when: Trial ends and the first charge succeeds, or a no-trial checkout completes
subscription.activated.description: The customer has paid. Grant access now.
subscription.activated.signature_verification: Every webhook request carries an X-Tori-Signature header — HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body, hex-encoded and prefixed "sha256=", using the signing secret shown once when the endpoint was registered. Verify it with a timing-safe comparison before trusting the payload.

payment.succeeded.fires_when: Any charge clears — a normal renewal, or a dunning recovery
payment.succeeded.description: A renewal charge succeeded. Access was already granted at subscription.activated and stays on.
payment.succeeded.signature_verification: Every webhook request carries an X-Tori-Signature header — HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body, hex-encoded and prefixed "sha256=", using the signing secret shown once when the endpoint was registered. Verify it with a timing-safe comparison before trusting the payload.

payment.failed.fires_when: A charge attempt fails — first failure, or any retry
payment.failed.description: A charge failed. Don't restrict access on the first payment.failed alone — the first failure enters a silent 48-hour grace retry. Watch for dunning.exhausted to know when recovery has genuinely run out.
payment.failed.signature_verification: Every webhook request carries an X-Tori-Signature header — HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body, hex-encoded and prefixed "sha256=", using the signing secret shown once when the endpoint was registered. Verify it with a timing-safe comparison before trusting the payload.

payment.action_required.fires_when: Recovery escalates to the manual pay-link rail — card and mandate retries are both exhausted
payment.action_required.description: Automatic recovery has stopped. Email or message the customer the included pay_link directly, or surface it in your product — this is the last rail before dunning.exhausted.
payment.action_required.signature_verification: Every webhook request carries an X-Tori-Signature header — HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body, hex-encoded and prefixed "sha256=", using the signing secret shown once when the endpoint was registered. Verify it with a timing-safe comparison before trusting the payload.

dunning.started.fires_when: The first scheduled dunning retry fires, after the silent grace-period retry has also failed
dunning.started.description: Retries are underway on the payday-aligned schedule (Day 3, 7, 14, 21).
dunning.started.signature_verification: Every webhook request carries an X-Tori-Signature header — HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body, hex-encoded and prefixed "sha256=", using the signing secret shown once when the endpoint was registered. Verify it with a timing-safe comparison before trusting the payload.

dunning.recovered.fires_when: A retry succeeds after at least one prior failure
dunning.recovered.description: The customer paid on a retry. Restore full access if you restricted it on an earlier payment.failed or dunning.started.
dunning.recovered.signature_verification: Every webhook request carries an X-Tori-Signature header — HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body, hex-encoded and prefixed "sha256=", using the signing secret shown once when the endpoint was registered. Verify it with a timing-safe comparison before trusting the payload.

dunning.exhausted.fires_when: Every configured retry attempt has failed — the subscription moves to SUSPENDED
dunning.exhausted.description: Recovery has genuinely run out on every automatic rail. Revoke access now.
dunning.exhausted.signature_verification: Every webhook request carries an X-Tori-Signature header — HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body, hex-encoded and prefixed "sha256=", using the signing secret shown once when the endpoint was registered. Verify it with a timing-safe comparison before trusting the payload.

subscription.cancelled.fires_when: A cancellation completes — either immediately, or when a deferred end-of-period cancellation reaches its period end
subscription.cancelled.signature_verification: Every webhook request carries an X-Tori-Signature header — HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body, hex-encoded and prefixed "sha256=", using the signing secret shown once when the endpoint was registered. Verify it with a timing-safe comparison before trusting the payload.

subscription.paused.fires_when: A customer or operator pauses a subscription
subscription.paused.description: Billing has stopped. Whether to restrict access while paused is your call.
subscription.paused.signature_verification: Every webhook request carries an X-Tori-Signature header — HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body, hex-encoded and prefixed "sha256=", using the signing secret shown once when the endpoint was registered. Verify it with a timing-safe comparison before trusting the payload.

subscription.resumed.description: Billing has restarted, on a fresh period starting now.
subscription.resumed.signature_verification: Every webhook request carries an X-Tori-Signature header — HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body, hex-encoded and prefixed "sha256=", using the signing secret shown once when the endpoint was registered. Verify it with a timing-safe comparison before trusting the payload.

subscription.suspended.fires_when: A subscription is suspended after dunning exhaustion, or by an operator directly
subscription.suspended.description: Access should end now — equivalent in effect to dunning.exhausted, but also covers operator-initiated suspension outside the dunning flow.
subscription.suspended.signature_verification: Every webhook request carries an X-Tori-Signature header — HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body, hex-encoded and prefixed "sha256=", using the signing secret shown once when the endpoint was registered. Verify it with a timing-safe comparison before trusting the payload.

trial.ending_soon.description: Give the customer a heads-up before their card is charged for the first time.
trial.ending_soon.signature_verification: Every webhook request carries an X-Tori-Signature header — HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body, hex-encoded and prefixed "sha256=", using the signing secret shown once when the endpoint was registered. Verify it with a timing-safe comparison before trusting the payload.

pay_link.paid.fires_when: A customer pays a payment link — via your own Platform API checkout call, or the bare shareable link directly
pay_link.paid.description: This is the only way to know a payment link was paid — it isn't a subscription, so it never fires subscription.activated or payment.succeeded. Note: the event_type string Tori actually dispatches is payment_link.paid (underscore, not pay_link) — documented here as pay_link.paid to match the requested spec, but match on payment_link.paid in your handler.
pay_link.paid.signature_verification: Every webhook request carries an X-Tori-Signature header — HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body, hex-encoded and prefixed "sha256=", using the signing secret shown once when the endpoint was registered. Verify it with a timing-safe comparison before trusting the payload.

payout.completed.fires_when: A requested bank transfer payout is confirmed by Nomba
payout.completed.description: Your payout landed. Useful if you reconcile payouts against your own accounting system automatically.
payout.completed.signature_verification: Every webhook request carries an X-Tori-Signature header — HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body, hex-encoded and prefixed "sha256=", using the signing secret shown once when the endpoint was registered. Verify it with a timing-safe comparison before trusting the payload.

payout.failed.fires_when: A requested bank transfer payout fails on Nomba's side
payout.failed.description: The transfer didn't go through — check failure_reason.
payout.failed.signature_verification: Every webhook request carries an X-Tori-Signature header — HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body, hex-encoded and prefixed "sha256=", using the signing secret shown once when the endpoint was registered. Verify it with a timing-safe comparison before trusting the payload.
